growth_records.medium: DSMZ 756c medium (mineral NOB medium; nitrite as sole energy and N source)

up.units: umol N / mg protein / h (= mmol/g protein/h)
up.method: reported Vmax; whole-cell microrespirometry, Michaelis-Menten fit (43.7 +/- 1.3, n=3), cells grown at 10 mM nitrite pH 7.5, assayed pH 7.5

up.units: umol / mg protein / h (= mmol/g protein/h)
up.method: calculated from Vmax and stoichiometry O2:NO2- = 1:2 (Eq.2): 43.7 x 0.5 = 21.85

sec.units: umol N / mg protein / h (= mmol/g protein/h)
sec.method: calculated from 1:1 nitrite->nitrate stoichiometry (NO2- + 1/2 O2 -> NO3-): equal to nitrite Vmax = 43.7

yields.note: Km(app) is half-saturation affinity (uM), not a rate

growth_records.cite: Lee et al. Kinetic plasticity of nitrite-oxidizing bacteria containing cytoplasmic nitrite oxidoreductase. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag040. [PMC12998223]
growth_records.snippet: The mean apparent half-saturation constant (Km(app)) ... and the maximum oxidation rate (Vmax) of cells grown with 10 mM nitrite were 388.0 +/- 52.2 uM (n=3) and 43.7 +/- 1.3 umol N mg protein-1 h-1 (n=3) ... when tested at pH 5.5, the Km(a
growth_records.notes: Values explicit in Results (N. winogradskyi Nb-255 grown at 10 mM nitrite, pH 7.5). Reported: Vmax = 43.7 umol N/mg protein/h; Km(app) = 388.0 uM at test pH 7.5, and 19.2 uM at test pH 5.5 (same growth condition, different assay pH). Note umol/mg protein = mmol/g protein (identical numeric). O2 uptake DERIVED: from Eq.2 stoichiometry NO2- + 1/2 O2 -> NO3-, O2 = 0.5 x nitrite Vmax = 0.5 x 43.7 = 21.85 umol/mg protein/h. Nitrate secretion DERIVED: 1 nitrate per nitrite oxidized = 43.7 umol/mg protein/h. Could NOT convert to mmol/gDW/h: paper uses 5.7 g WET cell weight per g protein (not dry weight), so gDW conversion not derivable. No numeric growth rate (mu) or doubling time reported in main text (Fig 2 nitrite-oxidation activity in d-1 shown only graphically); growth stated qualitatively similar at 1 vs 10 mM at neutral pH. Temperature = optimal (Supplementary Table S1), not stated in main text -> null. Medium = DSMZ 756c, composition only in Supplementary -> composition empty, stated false. Vmax is maximum whole-cell nitrite-oxidation (catabolic uptake) activity, an appropriate substrate-uptake bound for GEM constraint.

growth_records.medium: DSMZ 756c medium (mineral NOB medium; nitrite as sole energy and N source)

up.units: umol N / mg protein / h (= mmol/g protein/h)
up.method: reported Vmax; whole-cell microrespirometry, Michaelis-Menten fit (68.8 +/- 2.5, n=3), cells grown at 1 mM nitrite pH 7.5, assayed pH 7.5

up.units: umol / mg protein / h (= mmol/g protein/h)
up.method: calculated from Vmax and stoichiometry O2:NO2- = 1:2 (Eq.2): 68.8 x 0.5 = 34.4

sec.units: umol N / mg protein / h (= mmol/g protein/h)
sec.method: calculated from 1:1 nitrite->nitrate stoichiometry: equal to nitrite Vmax = 68.8

yields.note: ao and Km(app) are affinity metrics, not rates; ao computed by authors as (Vmax/(cellular protein x 5.7)) x Km(app)

growth_records.cite: Lee et al. Kinetic plasticity of nitrite-oxidizing bacteria containing cytoplasmic nitrite oxidoreductase. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag040. [PMC12998223]
growth_records.snippet: cells grown with 1 mM nitrite surprisingly exhibited a lower Km(app) of 25.9 +/- 5.2 uM (n=3), but had a Vmax of 68.8 +/- 2.5 umol N mg protein-1 h-1 (n=3) ... grown at low nitrite (1 mM), had a high apparent affinity (Km(app) = 25.9 uM; sp
growth_records.notes: Values explicit in Results/Abstract (N. winogradskyi Nb-255 grown at 1 mM nitrite, pH 7.5). Reported: Vmax = 68.8 umol N/mg protein/h; Km(app) = 25.9 uM (test pH 7.5) and 20.4 uM (test pH 5.5, n=5, same growth condition); specific affinity ao = 440.5 L/g cells/h at pH 7.5. umol/mg protein = mmol/g protein. O2 uptake DERIVED: 0.5 x 68.8 = 34.4 umol/mg protein/h (Eq.2, O2:NO2-=1:2). Nitrate secretion DERIVED: 1:1 = 68.8 umol/mg protein/h. gDW conversion not derivable (paper's 5.7 factor is wet cell weight per g protein). No numeric growth rate/doubling time in main text; authors state growth rates at 1 and 10 mM nitrite (neutral pH) were similar. Vmax at test pH 5.5 not given as an explicit number in main text (only Km 20.4). Distinct growth condition from the 10 mM record (this is the high-affinity low-nitrite phenotype). Temperature and full medium composition only in Supplementary -> null / empty.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salts nitrifier medium (chemostat feed, as previously described, ref 56)
growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: Nitrobacter winogradskyi (Nb-255)... routinely cultivated at 30C in batch and chemostat culture... Chemostat cultures were maintained in steady state at a dilution rate of 0.015 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state: mu = D = 0.015 h-1 (verbatim). td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.015 = 46.2 h. 30C stated. Chemostat feed medium cited to ref 56, not composed here -> composition [] / stated false.

growth_records.medium: Experimental batch mineral salts medium (N. winogradskyi, nitrite)

up.method: initial 5 mM NaNO2; ~80% of NO2- consumed by 4 h (Fig 2A); no numeric specific rate stated

sec.method: product of nitrite oxidation, measured by HPLC (Fig 2A time course); no numeric specific rate stated
model simulation: to account for observed NOx peak N. winogradskyi would need ~130 nmol NO h-1 per 5 ml = 0.13 umol/h / 0.005 L = 0.026 mmol/L/h (dFBA-inferred, not measured)

growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: The medium for N. winogradskyi was the same formulation as the N. europaea medium except that it contained 5 mM NaNO2 instead of (NH4)2SO4. The pH... adjusted to 7.8. ...N. winogradskyi would need to produce NO at a maximum rate of approxim
growth_records.notes: Batch medium identical to N. europaea recipe but with 5 mM NaNO2 replacing (NH4)2SO4 (verbatim). Conditions 30C, pH 7.8, 200 rpm, closed batch 10 h, OD600 0.05 inoculum, aerobic. No batch mu reported -> null. Substrate NO2- initial 5 mM, ~80% consumed by 4 h (Fig 2A). NO 0.026 mmol/L/h = 130 nmol/h / 5 ml (0.005 L): 0.13e-3 mmol /0.005 L/h = 0.026 mmol/L/h, model-inferred maximum not a measured net flux. NOx peak 1.5 ppm and N2O 0.08 ppm are measured headspace concentrations. No gDW conversion available.
